forzaHorizon6ReleaseDate.title: Forza Horizon 6 Release Date, Steam Unlock, and Early Access
forzaHorizon6ReleaseDate.intro: Track the key launch dates for Forza Horizon 6 across Xbox, PC, Steam, Game Pass, and PlayStation 5. This guide highlights when the game comes out, when Early Access starts, and which platform gets access first.

items.label: Official Launch Date
items.description: Forza Horizon 6 launches on Xbox Series X|S and PC through the Microsoft Store, Xbox app, and Steam. Standard and Deluxe Edition players enter during the full launch window.

items.label: Steam Store Date
items.description: The Steam storefront displays May 18, 2026 for the Steam release and provides the live store countdown, purchase buttons, and edition options.

items.label: Premium Early Access
items.description: Forza Horizon 6 Premium Edition players and Premium Upgrade owners can start up to four days before the full launch.

items.label: Game Pass Launch
items.description: Forza Horizon 6 Standard Edition is included with Game Pass Ultimate and PC Game Pass on launch day.

items.label: PlayStation 5 Release
items.description: Forza Horizon 6 arrives on PlayStation 5 later in 2026, with wishlist access available through the PlayStation Store.

summary: Forza Horizon 6 launches first on Xbox Series X|S and PC.
Forza Horizon 6 Premium Early Access starts four days before the full launch.
Forza Horizon 6 Steam timing can display one day earlier because storefront timing is region-based.

forzaHorizon6SteamPreOrder.title: How to Pre-Order Forza Horizon 6 on Steam
forzaHorizon6SteamPreOrder.intro: Use this Forza Horizon 6 Steam guide to choose an edition, understand Advanced Access, collect the pre-order bonus, and review PC store requirements before launch.

steps.title: Forza Horizon 6 Steam Store Page
steps.description: Open the Forza Horizon 6 Steam app page and use the wishlist, follow, or pre-purchase options. Steam lists Xbox Game Studios as publisher and Playground Games as developer.

steps.title: Forza Horizon 6 Steam Edition Choice
steps.description: Choose Standard Edition at $69.99, Deluxe Edition at $99.99, or Premium Edition at $119.99 depending on launch access and included content.

steps.title: Forza Horizon 6 Advanced Access Check
steps.description: Steam labels Advanced Access for the Premium Edition, while the Forza launch guide describes Premium Early Access as up to four days before the May 19 launch.

steps.title: Forza Horizon 6 Pre-Order Bonus
steps.description: Players who purchase a full edition or the Premium Upgrade before launch receive the exclusive pre-tuned Ferrari J50, redeemable from the in-game Autoshow when Forza Horizon 6 releases.

steps.title: Forza Horizon 6 Steam Feature Review
steps.description: Review Steam features such as single-player, online PvP, online co-op, Family Sharing, Windows 10 22H2, DirectX 12, 16 GB RAM, broadband internet, and SSD storage.

forzaHorizon6EditionsAndPrice.title: Forza Horizon 6 Editions, Prices, and Included Content
forzaHorizon6EditionsAndPrice.intro: Compare Forza Horizon 6 Standard, Deluxe, Premium Edition, and Premium Upgrade before buying. The main decision is whether you want only the full game, extra cars, or the full Premium package with Early Access and expansions.

includes: Pre-order Ferrari J50 bonus when purchased before launch
items.bestFor: Forza Horizon 6 players who want the base game or plan to play through Game Pass.

includes: Car Pass with 30 cars delivered weekly
items.bestFor: Forza Horizon 6 players who want extra car content without Premium Early Access.

includes: Up to four days Premium Early Access
Italian Passion Car Pack after launch
items.bestFor: Forza Horizon 6 players who want the earliest access and the broadest content bundle.

includes: Up to four days Premium Early Access
Italian Passion Car Pack after launch
items.bestFor: Forza Horizon 6 Game Pass members or Standard and Deluxe owners who want Premium benefits without buying another full edition.

forzaHorizon6GamePass.title: Is Forza Horizon 6 on Game Pass?
forzaHorizon6GamePass.intro: Forza Horizon 6 Standard Edition is included with Game Pass Ultimate and PC Game Pass on May 19, 2026. Game Pass players can also buy the Premium Upgrade for Early Access and Premium add-ons.

items.label: Game Pass Availability
items.description: Forza Horizon 6 Standard Edition joins Game Pass Ultimate and PC Game Pass at launch on May 19, 2026.

items.label: Console Access
items.description: Forza Horizon 6 console access is listed for Xbox Series X|S through Game Pass Ultimate, with Xbox Cloud Gaming included as part of the membership path.

items.label: PC Access
items.value: PC Game Pass or Game Pass Ultimate
items.description: Forza Horizon 6 PC players can access the game through the Xbox app and Microsoft Store with an eligible subscription.

items.label: Premium Upgrade
items.description: Forza Horizon 6 Game Pass players can purchase the Premium Upgrade to play up to four days early and receive Premium Edition add-ons.

items.label: Xbox Play Anywhere
items.description: Forza Horizon 6 Microsoft Store and Xbox purchases support Xbox Play Anywhere across eligible Xbox console and PC devices.

items.label: Steam Version
items.description: Forza Horizon 6 on Steam is sold as a separate storefront purchase with Standard, Deluxe, and Premium pre-purchase options.

forzaHorizon6Ps5Release.title: Forza Horizon 6 PS5 Release
forzaHorizon6Ps5Release.intro: Forza Horizon 6 is coming to PlayStation 5 later in 2026 after the Xbox Series X|S and PC launch. This platform guide covers PS5 wishlist access, launch timing, ownership, DLC purchases, and cross-save support.

items.question: Is Forza Horizon 6 coming to PS5?
items.answer: Yes. Forza Horizon 6 has a PlayStation Store page from Microsoft Corporation, and PlayStation 5 players can add the game to their wishlist.
highlights: Forza Horizon 6 platform: PlayStation 5
Forza Horizon 6 publisher: Microsoft Corporation
Forza Horizon 6 PS Store status: Announced

items.question: When is the Forza Horizon 6 PS5 version launching?
items.answer: The Forza Horizon 6 PS5 version is planned for later in 2026. Xbox Series X|S and PC launch first on May 19, 2026, with Premium Edition early access beginning May 15.
highlights: Forza Horizon 6 Xbox and PC launch: May 19, 2026
Forza Horizon 6 Premium early access: May 15, 2026
Forza Horizon 6 PS5 release window: Later in 2026

items.question: Can PS5 players wishlist or pre-order Forza Horizon 6?
items.answer: Forza Horizon 6 PS5 players can add the game to their wishlist on the PlayStation Store. Steam, Xbox, and Microsoft Store PC versions are available for pre-order.
highlights: Forza Horizon 6 PS5: Wishlist available
Forza Horizon 6 Steam: Pre-purchase available
Forza Horizon 6 Xbox and Microsoft Store PC: Pre-order available

items.question: Is Forza Horizon 6 PS5 included with another platform purchase?
items.answer: No. Forza Horizon 6 on PS5 is a standalone product. Steam is also treated as a standalone product, while Xbox Play Anywhere covers digital Xbox console and Microsoft Store PC versions.
highlights: Forza Horizon 6 PS5 ownership is separate
Forza Horizon 6 Steam ownership is separate
Forza Horizon 6 Xbox Play Anywhere covers Xbox console and Microsoft Store PC

items.question: Will Forza Horizon 6 support cross-play and cross-save on PS5?
items.answer: Forza Horizon 6 supports cross-play and cross-save between PC and console versions, including Xbox Series X|S, Xbox on PC, Steam, SteamOS devices, PC handhelds, and the PS5 version when it arrives.
highlights: Forza Horizon 6 cross-play: PC and console
Forza Horizon 6 cross-save: Xbox, PC, Steam, SteamOS, PC handhelds, and PS5
Forza Horizon 6 handheld progress support: Steam Deck and Xbox ROG Ally

items.question: Do Forza Horizon 6 DLC purchases carry over to PS5?
items.answer: Forza Horizon 6 downloadable content such as Car Packs and Expansions must be purchased on each platform where you want to access that content. Xbox Play Anywhere-supported devices are the exception for Xbox console and Microsoft Store PC access.
highlights: Forza Horizon 6 DLC is platform-specific
Forza Horizon 6 PS5 DLC purchases are separate
Forza Horizon 6 Xbox Play Anywhere covers supported Xbox and Microsoft Store PC devices

forzaHorizon6SystemRequirements.title: Forza Horizon 6 System Requirements
forzaHorizon6SystemRequirements.intro: Check Forza Horizon 6 minimum and recommended PC specs for Steam, including Windows version, CPU, GPU, memory, DirectX 12, broadband internet, SSD storage, handheld support, and graphics features.

items.notes: Forza Horizon 6 requires a 64-bit processor and operating system.
Forza Horizon 6 recommended specs target a newer mid-range CPU class for smoother open-world racing performance.
Forza Horizon 6 lists 16 GB RAM for both minimum and recommended PC configurations.

items.minimum: Nvidia GTX 1650, AMD RX 6500 XT, or Intel Arc A380
items.recommended: NVIDIA RTX 3060 Ti, AMD RX 6700 XT, or Intel Arc A580
items.notes: Forza Horizon 6 compatible graphics cards can use enhanced ray tracing features.
Forza Horizon 6 requires DirectX 12 on PC.
Forza Horizon 6 online features, multiplayer, and connected services require internet access.
Forza Horizon 6 Steam requirements list SSD storage as required.

items.minimum: 4K HDR, enhanced ray tracing features, high uncapped framerates, and ultrawide monitor support
items.recommended: Use compatible hardware for ray-traced reflections, RTGI, high framerates, and advanced graphics settings
items.notes: Forza Horizon 6 PC graphics settings can be changed without restarting the game, with live previews for many settings.

items.minimum: NVIDIA DLSS, AMD FSR, and Intel XeSS support on compatible hardware
items.recommended: NVIDIA DLSS 4, AMD FSR 4 or 3, and Intel XeSS 2.1
items.notes: Forza Horizon 6 supports advanced upscaling and frame generation options on compatible graphics hardware.

items.minimum: PC handheld support, including Steam Deck and Xbox ROG Ally
items.recommended: Use cross-save to continue progress across supported PC, console, SteamOS, and handheld devices
items.notes: Forza Horizon 6 supports cross-save across every platform where the game can be played.

items.recommended: Use benchmark mode to test performance after changing graphics settings
items.notes: Forza Horizon 6 includes a PC benchmark mode for consistent performance testing.

forzaHorizon6JapanMap.title: Forza Horizon 6 Japan Map
forzaHorizon6JapanMap.intro: The Forza Horizon 6 Japan map is built around the contrast of urban and rural Japan, modern and traditional scenery, diverse biomes, seasonal changes, mountain roads, and world-famous route inspirations.

items.question: What is the Forza Horizon 6 map location?
items.answer: Forza Horizon 6 takes place in Japan, bringing together scenic rural landscapes, dense urban driving, mountain roads, diverse biomes, and open-world racing routes.
highlights: Forza Horizon 6 main setting: Japan
Forza Horizon 6 world style: Horizon-style interpretation of Japan
Forza Horizon 6 core contrast: Rural and urban, modern and traditional

items.question: How big is the Forza Horizon 6 map?
items.answer: Forza Horizon 6 features the largest map in a Horizon game yet, with strong emphasis on verticality, density, diverse biomes, and seasonal driving experiences.
highlights: Forza Horizon 6 largest Horizon map yet
Forza Horizon 6 most dense Horizon map yet
Forza Horizon 6 built for verticality and varied driving

items.question: What areas are shown in the Forza Horizon 6 full map reveal?
items.answer: The Forza Horizon 6 full map reveal presents Horizon Japan from the downtown streets of Tokyo City to the snowy Japanese Alps, giving players a broad mix of city roads, mountains, and scenic exploration routes.
highlights: Forza Horizon 6 Tokyo City
Forza Horizon 6 snowy Japanese Alps
Forza Horizon 6 mountain areas
Forza Horizon 6 rural and natural landscapes

items.question: Which real-world routes inspired the Forza Horizon 6 Japan map?
items.answer: Forza Horizon 6 includes roads inspired by the C1 loop and Gingko Avenue, as well as mountain passes inspired by Mt. Haruna and Bandai Azuma.
highlights: Forza Horizon 6 C1 loop-inspired roads
Forza Horizon 6 Gingko Avenue-inspired roads
Forza Horizon 6 Mt. Haruna-inspired mountain passes
Forza Horizon 6 Bandai Azuma-inspired mountain passes

items.question: Does the Forza Horizon 6 Japan map include seasons and biomes?
items.answer: Yes. The Forza Horizon 6 Japan map includes diverse biomes and seasonality, combining urban roads, rural landscapes, mountain scenery, and natural environments across a varied open world.
highlights: Forza Horizon 6 dynamic seasonal feel
Forza Horizon 6 urban and rural biomes
Forza Horizon 6 mountain and natural regions
Forza Horizon 6 dense city exploration

items.question: How does the Forza Horizon 6 map connect to progression?
items.answer: Forza Horizon 6 players start as tourists, qualify through the Horizon Invitational, rise through faster car classes, earn Wristbands, complete the Collection Journal, and work toward Horizon Legend status.
highlights: Forza Horizon 6 tourist starting point
Forza Horizon 6 Horizon Invitational
Forza Horizon 6 Wristband progression
Forza Horizon 6 Collection Journal
Forza Horizon 6 Legend Island access

forzaHorizon6TokyoCity.title: Forza Horizon 6 Tokyo City
forzaHorizon6TokyoCity.intro: Tokyo City is one of the biggest reasons players are searching for Forza Horizon 6. This city guide highlights scale, road density, districts, shortcuts, vertical driving, car culture, and open-world multiplayer activities.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 Largest Urban Area
items.description: Tokyo City is the largest urban environment ever featured in a Forza Horizon game and is five times larger than Forza Horizon 5's Guanajuato.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 Iconic Downtown Streets
items.description: Tokyo City includes iconic downtown streets designed around neon lights, busy roads, layered scenery, and fast exploration with friends.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 Suburbs and Shortcuts
items.description: Tokyo City includes suburban roads, corners, and shortcuts built to make every route feel fun to drive and easy to explore.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 Docks and Industrial Districts
items.description: Tokyo City lets players challenge themselves in docks and industrial zones, adding a different road feel from downtown and suburban areas.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 Dense and Vertical Road Design
items.description: Forza Horizon 6 emphasizes density and verticality, making Tokyo City one of the most detailed and layered environments in the game.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 Car Enthusiast Hub
items.description: Tokyo City is positioned as a major car culture hub, with Car Meets, Drag Meets, Time Attack Circuits, and open-world social driving activities.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 JDM and Street Driving Focus
items.description: The Tokyo setting supports Forza Horizon 6 Japanese car culture, including JDM classics, Touge Battles, rare Aftermarket Cars, and authentic car stories.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 Co-op City Exploration
items.description: Tokyo City can be explored solo or with friends, with multiplayer activities designed to happen naturally in the open world.

forzaHorizon6CarList.title: Forza Horizon 6 Car List: Confirmed Cars, Classes, Cover Cars, and Add-Ons
forzaHorizon6CarList.subtitle: Browse officially revealed Forza Horizon 6 cars, including cover vehicles, JDM icons, Forza Edition models, Time Attack machines, promotional cars, and car-pack additions.
forzaHorizon6CarList.intro: Forza Horizon 6 launches with more than 550 cars to collect and customize, with the roster balanced across performance classes and a new R Class for track-focused cars. This Forza Horizon 6 car list focuses on high-interest confirmed highlights from the current official roster.

items.highlight: Video game debut and one of the two Forza Horizon 6 cover vehicles.
The second Forza Horizon 6 cover vehicle, representing Japan's off-road and adventure side.

Modern JDM performance icon for high-speed Forza Horizon 6 road and track builds.
Classic Mk4 Supra for Forza Horizon 6 JDM, tuning, drag, and street-race builds.
AE86 icon for Forza Horizon 6 touge, drift, and lightweight classic builds.

items.car: 1985 Toyota Sprinter Trueno GT Apex Forza Edition
items.highlight: Track-spec Forza Horizon 6 Forza Edition version with extreme modifications.
Purpose-built Forza Horizon 6 time-attack Forza Edition kei car.

items.car: 1999 Dodge Viper GTS ACR Forza Edition
items.highlight: VIP Forza Horizon 6 Forza Edition with off-road-focused tuning.
JDM roadster also featured as a Forza Horizon 6 Aftermarket Car with a Rocket Bunny body kit.
Kei sports car featured as a Forza Horizon 6 Aftermarket Car with Mazdaspeed upgrades.
Featured as a Forza Horizon 6 Aftermarket Car with Duke Dynamics modifications.
One of the Forza Horizon 6 Treasure Cars found by exploring Japan.

Track-focused Forza Horizon 6 Skyline built for Time Attack Circuits.

forzaHorizon6JdmCarsAndCustomization.title: Forza Horizon 6 JDM Cars, Body Kits, Forza Aero, Liveries, Rims, and Engine Swaps
forzaHorizon6JdmCarsAndCustomization.subtitle: Build JDM classics and modern Japanese icons with expanded customization, new paint options, window liveries, Forza Aero improvements, and rare car-discovery systems.
forzaHorizon6JdmCarsAndCustomization.intro: Forza Horizon 6 leans into Japanese car culture with fan-favorite JDM classics, updated steering animations, new cosmetic tire wear, and deeper personalization. The customization flow covers body kits, aero parts, window liveries, rims, paint materials, Kei-car engine swaps, Aftermarket Cars, Treasure Cars, and Forza Edition builds.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 JDM roster highlights
items.description: Official Japanese-brand highlights include the 2025 GR GT Prototype, 2025 Toyota Land Cruiser, 2024 Nissan GT-R NISMO, 2020 Toyota GR Supra, 1998 Toyota Supra RZ, 1992 Honda NSX-R, 1990 Mazda Savanna RX-7, 1985 Toyota Sprinter Trueno GT Apex, and 1994 Subaru Vivio RX-R.
items.bestFor: Players searching for Forza Horizon 6 JDM cars, drift builds, touge cars, and Japanese garage themes.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 Forza Aero upgrades
items.description: Forza Aero is updated so aero parts can be set up per car rather than using the same generic front and rear setup. Players can also paint elements of Forza Aero bumpers and rear wings independently, including materials, supports, and tow hooks.
items.bestFor: Forza Horizon 6 track builds, clean show cars, and detailed livery projects.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 body kits and aftermarket brands
items.description: Select vehicles support new body kits, with aftermarket brands including Liberty Walk, Rocket Bunny, and Origin Lab. Official examples include a 2003 Honda S2000 with a Rocket Bunny body kit and a 2015 McLaren 570S Coupe with Duke Dynamics modifications.
items.bestFor: Forza Horizon 6 widebody builds, street style, drift cars, and Car Meet showcases.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 window liveries and new vinyls
items.description: The Livery Editor adds the requested ability to paint liveries on windows. New pre-made in-world branding vinyls include PI Class logos and Wristband icons, with additional decals unlockable through the Collection Journal.
items.bestFor: Forza Horizon 6 race replicas, time-attack liveries, window banners, and custom crew branding.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 paint materials and favorites
items.description: The paint workflow is streamlined so players can switch between gloss, semi-gloss, matte, and metallic finishes more easily. New favorites help save colors and materials, including metal flake and candy paint options.
items.bestFor: Forza Horizon 6 players who frequently tune car colorways and garage display builds.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 rims and stance details
items.description: Forza Horizon 6 adds more than 100 new rims and lets players install different rims on the front and rear of a car. Tire visuals have also been refreshed, with new cosmetic tire wear and updated tire patterns for all tires except off-road tires.
items.bestFor: Forza Horizon 6 street builds, stance builds, drift cars, and photo-mode-ready garage setups.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 kei car motorcycle engine swaps
items.description: Kei cars can be overhauled with motorcycle engine swaps, opening a lightweight high-revving build path for Japan's smallest cars.
items.bestFor: Forza Horizon 6 experimental builds, compact track cars, and unusual speed challenges.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 Forza Edition extreme builds
items.description: Forza Edition cars return with more extreme modifications. Official examples include a track-spec 1985 Toyota Sprinter, a 1994 Subaru Vivio RX-R built for time attack, and a VIP 1999 Dodge Viper GTS ACR with off-road tuning.
items.bestFor: Forza Horizon 6 collectors who want special variants and high-impact garage pieces.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 Aftermarket Cars
items.description: Aftermarket Cars are parked around Japan and can be test-driven or bought, often at a discount. They appear near useful activities such as Festival races and Time Attack Circuits, making them a natural way to try cars that fit nearby events.
items.bestFor: Forza Horizon 6 players who want to discover useful cars while exploring rather than buying everything from the Autoshow.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 Treasure Cars and Barn Finds
items.description: Treasure Cars are abandoned vehicles found through photo clues from Mei, while Barn Finds return through clues unlocked by collecting Stamps for the Collection Journal. Confirmed Treasure Car examples include the 1991 Nissan Figaro, 1985 Nissan Safari Turbo, and 1969 Dodge Charger R/T.
items.bestFor: Forza Horizon 6 explorers, collectors, and players who enjoy map-based car hunts.

forzaHorizon6TrailerAndGameplay.subtitle: Watch the official reveal, Tokyo City showcase, initial drive gameplay, and Forza channel updates for Japan, car culture, and open-world racing footage.
forzaHorizon6TrailerAndGameplay.intro: Forza Horizon 6 video interest centers on the Japan reveal, Tokyo City, the first gameplay drive, and official feature videos. This module groups the core videos users are most likely to search before launch.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 Official Teaser Trailer | Tokyo Game Show 2025
items.description: The reveal trailer introduces Horizon Japan and confirms the highly requested Japan setting for Forza Horizon 6.
items.watchReason: Best first video for users searching Forza Horizon 6 trailer or reveal trailer.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 Official Initial Drive Gameplay
items.description: The first official gameplay drive shows the opening minutes of Forza Horizon 6, including fast-paced car switching, Japan scenery, and early festival spectacle.
items.watchReason: Best video for users searching Forza Horizon 6 gameplay or first gameplay footage.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 Tokyo City | Biome Showcase
items.description: This showcase focuses on Tokyo City, the largest urban environment ever built for a Forza Horizon game, including dense roads, verticality, districts, and city driving atmosphere.
items.watchReason: Best match for Forza Horizon 6 Tokyo trailer, city map, and Japan open-world searches.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 The Art of Driving
items.description: The Art of Driving series explores the Japanese artists and cultural references that shape the murals, visual identity, and car-culture atmosphere of Forza Horizon 6.
items.watchReason: Useful for users interested in Forza Horizon 6 Japanese culture, murals, and artistic direction.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 Official Forza YouTube Channel
items.description: The official channel collects Forza Horizon 6 trailers, biome showcases, garage features, car culture videos, and post-reveal updates.
items.watchReason: Best ongoing source for newly published Forza Horizon 6 official gameplay clips.

forzaHorizon6BeginnerGuide.title: Forza Horizon 6 Beginner Guide: Tourist Start, Wristbands, Collection Journal, and Legend Island
forzaHorizon6BeginnerGuide.subtitle: Learn how the early campaign works, how to qualify for the Horizon Festival, how Wristbands and Stamps progress, and what to prioritize when exploring Japan.
forzaHorizon6BeginnerGuide.intro: Forza Horizon 6 starts differently from previous Horizon games: you arrive in Japan as a tourist instead of a superstar. The early loop is built around proving yourself, qualifying for the Horizon Festival, earning Wristbands, collecting Stamps, and using the Collection Journal to turn exploration into steady rewards.

steps.title: Forza Horizon 6 tourist start in Japan
steps.description: Your campaign begins as a festival tourist with no carried-over fame from previous Horizon games. Mei, an experienced Japanese car builder, and Jordy, a motorsports enthusiast, help you discover Japan and work toward joining the Horizon Festival.
steps.tip: Treat the Forza Horizon 6 opening as both a tutorial and a world-introduction route: learn the roads, listen to the guidance, and sample different surfaces before chasing speed.

steps.title: Forza Horizon 6 Horizon Qualifiers
steps.description: To join the Horizon Festival, complete the Horizon Qualifiers and then the Horizon Invitational. This earns your first Wristband and moves you from tourist status into the official Festival progression path.
steps.tip: Focus on clean driving and learning Forza Horizon 6 event types first; the campaign gradually introduces faster cars and more specific restrictions.

steps.title: Forza Horizon 6 Festival races
steps.description: Festival races are curated around specific car sets in Road, Dirt, and Cross Country events. Each new Wristband opens more events around Japan and moves the campaign toward faster car themes.
steps.tip: Do not rush to hypercars for Forza Horizon 6 Festival progression; official Festival events use structured restrictions.

steps.title: Forza Horizon 6 Wristband progression
steps.description: Festival Events, PR Stunts, and Skills contribute to your next Wristband. When you have enough progress, complete a Wristband Event such as a Showcase or Horizon Rush obstacle course.
steps.tip: Forza Horizon 6 Horizon Rush courses test speed and precision with splits, star targets, and locations such as Tokyo City Docks, Sotoyama Ski Resort, and Irokawa Space Center.

steps.title: Forza Horizon 6 Race Customizer
steps.description: After completing a Festival Event once, Race Customizer unlocks for that event. You can adjust Drivatars up to 11, season, weather, time of day, time progression, laps, Rewind, camera lock, traffic, and car restrictions.
steps.tip: Replay completed Forza Horizon 6 events with your own restrictions to test builds, create themed races, or play the same setup with a Convoy.

steps.title: Forza Horizon 6 Discover Japan route
steps.description: Outside the Festival path, Discover Japan focuses on earning Stamps through photos, food delivery, car collecting, Horizon Stories, Touge Battles, and night-time Street Races. This path lets you explore at your own pace.
steps.tip: Forza Horizon 6 Street Races and Touge Battles are useful when you want to drive cars that are not currently allowed in Festival Events.

steps.title: Forza Horizon 6 Collection Journal
steps.description: The Collection Journal tracks campaign progress across Horizon Festival and Discover Japan. It also records discoveries such as cars, houses, landmarks, mascots, and photos, then lets you redeem rewards including cars, credits, and cosmetic items.
steps.tip: Use the Forza Horizon 6 Collection Journal as your main checklist when you feel unsure what to do next.

steps.title: Forza Horizon 6 homes, garages, Barn Finds, and The Estate
steps.description: Collecting Stamps unlocks Player Houses with Customizable Garages, Barn Find Rumors, and The Estate, a mountain valley where you can build and decorate directly in the open world.
steps.tip: Prioritize Forza Horizon 6 Stamps if you care about garage display, map exploration, and long-term collection rewards.

steps.title: Forza Horizon 6 Legend Island
steps.description: Earning all 7 Festival Wristbands makes you a Horizon Legend and unlocks Legend Island, an exclusive region with the Legend Island Circuit, unique events, and the Colossus, the longest Goliath event that loops the full map on the freeway.
steps.tip: Forza Horizon 6 Legend Island is designed as a major campaign milestone and a strong endgame target for R Class cars.

forzaHorizon6TougeBattlesAndTimeAttack.title: Forza Horizon 6 Touge Battles and Time Attack
forzaHorizon6TougeBattlesAndTimeAttack.subtitle: Break down Forza Horizon 6 race formats built around Japan's mountain roads, shared-world circuits, drag strips, night-time street racing, and leaderboard chasing.
forzaHorizon6TougeBattlesAndTimeAttack.intro: Forza Horizon 6 leans heavily into Japanese car culture with Touge Battles, Time Attack Circuits, Drag Meets, Street Races, and roads inspired by famous Japanese driving locations. These activities sit alongside the Horizon Festival campaign and Discover Japan path, giving players structured races, open-world challenges, leaderboard pressure, and free-roam-friendly competition.

items.label: Forza Horizon 6 Touge Battles
items.title: Night-time mountain pass races
items.content: Forza Horizon 6 Touge Battles are night-time races down Japanese mountain passes. They are part of the Discover Japan experience and connect directly to the game's Japanese car culture theme.
items.playerGoal: Master tight roads, elevation changes, and mountain-pass racing outside the stricter Horizon Festival race structure.
details: Just Getting Started: Complete your first Touge Battle

items.label: Forza Horizon 6 Time Attack Circuits
items.title: Shared-world hot laps with live leaderboard pressure
items.content: Forza Horizon 6 Time Attack challenges players to set the fastest lap time across grassroots circuits in the Shared World. Players start by driving through a gantry, then hot lap without loading screens while earning Credits and XP for completed laps.
items.playerGoal: Set a Current Best, chase split times, compare against friends in a Convoy Leaderboard, and compete on in-world seasonal leaderboards.
details: Another Lap Won't Hurt: Set a lap time at a Time Attack Circuit
Track Day: Set a lap time at a Time Attack Circuit while driving any Track Toy

items.label: Forza Horizon 6 Drag Meets
items.title: Shared-world launch battles across three drag strips
items.content: Forza Horizon 6 Drag Meets test car performance across varied drag strips with synchronized tree lights. Confirmed Drag Strip locations include Festival Site 1K, Irokawa Space Center 1/4 Mile, and Airfield 1/2 Mile.
items.playerGoal: Ready up at the start grid, launch with other players, tune for acceleration and top speed, and chase Convoy or weekly in-world leaderboard times.
details: Fresh Tire Smoke: Complete a Drag Meet
Up to 12 players per Drag Meet

items.label: Forza Horizon 6 Street Races
items.title: Night-time racing for cars outside Festival restrictions
items.content: Forza Horizon 6 Street Races appear within the Discover Japan path and are positioned as a place to drive cars that may not yet qualify for official Horizon Festival events.
items.playerGoal: Race freely in Japan at night while progressing outside the main Wristband race structure.

items.label: Forza Horizon 6 Japanese Road Inspiration
items.title: C1 loop, Gingko Avenue, Mt. Haruna, and Bandai Azuma influences
items.content: Forza Horizon 6 Japan features roads inspired by the C1 loop, Gingko Avenue, and mountain passes such as Mt. Haruna and Bandai Azuma. This gives the race-type mix a stronger Japan-specific identity than a generic open-world racing layout.
items.playerGoal: Use the urban expressway-style roads, neon city districts, rural routes, and mountain areas to build different driving routines.

items.label: Forza Horizon 6 Time Attack Car Pack
items.title: Eight launch cars tuned for Time Attack driving
items.content: The Forza Horizon 6 Time Attack Car Pack adds 8 cars to the garage at release, built around mastering Time Attack Circuits around Japan.
items.playerGoal: Use purpose-built WTAC-style cars for circuit practice, leaderboard attempts, and Time Attack-focused garage planning.
details: 1990 Mitsubishi #269 Attacking the Clock Racing Minicab Time Attack

forzaHorizon6MultiplayerAndCrossSave.title: Forza Horizon 6 Multiplayer and Cross Save
forzaHorizon6MultiplayerAndCrossSave.subtitle: Forza Horizon 6 supports co-op play, shared open-world activities, returning Horizon Multiplayer modes, and cross-save across PC and console versions.
forzaHorizon6MultiplayerAndCrossSave.intro: Multiplayer in Forza Horizon 6 is built around seamless shared-world activities rather than waiting through loading screens for every social moment. Players can join Car Meets, complete Drag Meets and Time Attack Circuits, earn LINK Skills together, compete in Spec Racing Championships, and carry progress across the supported PC and console ecosystem.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 Co-op Campaign and Convoys
items.description: The Forza Horizon 6 campaign is playable solo or in co-op with friends. When replaying Festival Events, a Convoy leader can shape an event setup through Race Customizer options such as Drivatars, season, weather, time of day, laps, Rewind, camera lock, traffic, and car restrictions.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 Car Meets
items.description: Forza Horizon 6 Car Meets return as Shared Open World spaces where players can drive past, see real live players, and join instantly without loading screens. Confirmed locations include the Horizon Festival Site, Okuibuki Parking Lot in the alpine, and Daikoku in Tokyo City, with temporary Car Meets planned for the Live Program.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 LINK Skills
items.description: Forza Horizon 6 LINK Skills reward players when they bank skills at the same time as another player, making tandem drifting, coordinated jumps, and group skill chains part of the shared-world progression loop.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 Time Attack and Drag Meets
items.description: Forza Horizon 6 Time Attack Circuits and Drag Meets are seamless open-world activities with Convoy Leaderboards and in-world leaderboards. Drag Meets support up to 12 players, while Time Attack lets any player arrive in the Shared World and chase lap times.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 Horizon Multiplayer Modes
items.description: Forza Horizon 6 includes returning Horizon Multiplayer modes such as The Eliminator and Hide & Seek, plus new Spec Racing Championships for more structured competitive racing.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 Cross-play and Cross-save
items.description: Forza Horizon 6 supports cross-play and cross-save between PC and console versions. Cross-save covers Xbox Series X|S, Xbox on PC, Steam, SteamOS devices, PC gaming handhelds, and the PlayStation 5 version coming later in 2026.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 Xbox Play Anywhere
items.description: Digital Forza Horizon 6 purchases through Xbox Series X|S or the Microsoft Store on PC support Xbox Play Anywhere, allowing play on Xbox console and PC with save profile and eligible DLC entitlements carried over within that ecosystem.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 Steam and PS5 Ownership
items.description: Forza Horizon 6 on Steam is a standalone product, and the PlayStation 5 version is also a standalone product. Paid downloadable content generally needs to be purchased on each platform where the player wants to access it, except where Xbox Play Anywhere entitlement rules apply.

forzaHorizon6TreasureCarsAndCollectionJo.title: Forza Horizon 6 Treasure Cars and Collection Journal
forzaHorizon6TreasureCarsAndCollectionJo.subtitle: Use the Forza Horizon 6 Collection Journal to track cars, houses, landmarks, mascots, Stamps, photos, Barn Finds, and Treasure Cars across Japan.
forzaHorizon6TreasureCarsAndCollectionJo.intro: The Collection Journal is the main exploration record for Forza Horizon 6. It tracks both Horizon Festival and Discover Japan progress, records what players find across Japan, stores photos attached to the Journal, and connects exploration to rewards such as cars, credits, cosmetics, Player Houses, Barn Find Rumors, and The Estate.

steps.title: Reveal Forza Horizon 6 Japan as you drive
steps.description: Explore the map naturally instead of only following fixed icons. Forza Horizon 6 introduces a fog-of-war style map reveal, so regions and activities become part of the road trip as players discover them.

steps.title: Earn Forza Horizon 6 Collection Journal Stamps
steps.description: Discover Japan focuses on earning Stamps inspired by Japan's stamp collecting culture. Players earn progress through activities such as taking photos, delivering food as a side hustle, collecting cars, and completing Horizon Stories.

steps.title: Use the Forza Horizon 6 Journal as a reward hub
steps.description: The Collection Journal is where players redeem unique rewards for completing specific activities and challenges. Rewards include cars, credits, and cosmetic items, and Journal photos can be saved as keepsakes or replaced later with better shots.

steps.title: Find Forza Horizon 6 Aftermarket Cars in the open world
steps.description: Aftermarket Cars are parked around Japan and can be test driven or added to the collection. Some appear near Festival Races that match campaign restrictions, while others sit near Time Attack Circuits with modifications suited for track driving.
targets: 2003 Honda S2000 with a Rocket Bunny body kit
1993 Autozam AZ-1 upgraded with Mazdaspeed parts
2015 McLaren 570S Coupe with Duke Dynamics modifications
hooks: Bargain Hunt: Purchase an Aftermarket Car

steps.title: Solve Forza Horizon 6 Treasure Car photo clues
steps.description: Mei sends photograph clues for abandoned Treasure Cars hidden through exploration. When players find a Treasure Car, they can restore it and drive it immediately or add it to the garage.
hooks: A Fine Addition To My Collection: Discover and claim a Treasure Car
Treasure Hunter: Discover and claim 9 Treasure Cars

steps.title: Unlock Forza Horizon 6 Barn Find Rumors through Stamps
steps.description: Barn Finds return alongside Aftermarket Cars and Treasure Cars. Barn Find clues are earned as players collect Stamps for the Collection Journal.

steps.title: Clear Forza Horizon 6 mascot, board, and regional collection goals
steps.description: Exploration also includes collectible mascots, bonus boards, regional activity goals, and story stars. These objectives turn free roam into a long-tail completion route after the main campaign milestones.

forzaHorizon6Achievements.title: Forza Horizon 6 Achievements: All 57 Goals and 1,000 Gamerscore
forzaHorizon6Achievements.subtitle: Track every Forza Horizon 6 Steam and Xbox achievement target across Wristbands, Stamps, Touge Battles, Time Attack, Treasure Cars, Car Meets, Horizon Play, and exploration.
forzaHorizon6Achievements.intro: Forza Horizon 6 launches with a full achievement list shared across Xbox and Steam versions. The goals cover the Horizon Festival campaign, Discover Japan Stamps, Collection Journal progress, car collecting, unique events, multiplayer activities, and open-world completion.

summary: Forza Horizon 6 includes 57 achievements worth 1,000 Gamerscore.
Forza Horizon 6 achievement goals cover campaign, Discover Japan, Collection Journal, cars, racing, multiplayer, and exploration.
Forza Horizon 6 completion routes should balance Wristbands, Stamps, Treasure Cars, Time Attack, Car Meets, and open-world collectibles.

faq.title: Forza Horizon 6 FAQ
faq.subtitle: Quick Forza Horizon 6 answers for launch date, Steam, Game Pass, Premium Early Access, and platform availability.

questions.question: When is the Forza Horizon 6 release date?
questions.answer: Forza Horizon 6 launches on Xbox Series X|S and PC on May 19, 2026, with the Steam store displaying May 18, 2026 for its storefront timing.

questions.question: Does Forza Horizon 6 have Premium Early Access?
questions.answer: Yes. Forza Horizon 6 Premium Edition and Premium Upgrade owners can start up to four days early beginning May 15, 2026.

questions.question: Is Forza Horizon 6 on Steam?
questions.answer: Yes. Forza Horizon 6 is available for Steam pre-purchase with Standard, Deluxe, and Premium Edition options.

questions.question: Is Forza Horizon 6 on Game Pass?
questions.answer: Yes. Forza Horizon 6 Standard Edition is included with Game Pass Ultimate and PC Game Pass on launch day.

questions.question: How much does Forza Horizon 6 cost on Steam?
questions.answer: Forza Horizon 6 Steam pricing lists Standard Edition at $69.99, Deluxe Edition at $99.99, and Premium Edition at $119.99.

questions.question: Is Forza Horizon 6 coming to PlayStation 5?
questions.answer: Yes. Forza Horizon 6 arrives on PlayStation 5 later in 2026 after the Xbox Series X|S and PC launch.
